desc: "I probably just parked it on the next block. Or maybe it's invisible today."
advice: Keep walking around the block in circles. It builds calf muscles.

desc: "I hope the thief rides it into a volcano."
advice: Scream into a pillow. Or write a strongly worded Yelp review for the sidewalk.

desc: "Walking is natural. Humans have walked for millennia. I love walking. It takes 45 minutes to get milk now."
advice: Invest in good shoes. You live in them now.

desc: "$2.75? For a ride that smells like unwashed gym socks?"
advice: Accept that public transit is your new chauffeur.

desc: "It was a nice bike. It belongs to the universe now. (And also Craig form Craigslist)."
advice: Buy a U-lock next time. A real one.

desc: "Wow, the bike fairy must have needed my specialized hybrid for an emergency!"
advice: Take deep breaths. The fairy isn't real.

desc: "Why did they leave the front wheel? It's a very nice wheel."
advice: Carry the wheel everywhere to remind you of your loss.

desc: "If I post enough flyers, they'll surely return it with an apology note."
advice: Use waterproof markers for the flyers. Tears will smudge them.

desc: "Look at that guy riding a bike. Does he know how lucky he is?"
advice: Stop glaring at cyclists. They can feel it.

desc: "I am buying a $50 rusted frame from the 80s that nobody will touch."
advice: Get a tetanus shot before your first ride.

desc: "I locked it for literally 45 seconds while I grabbed a coffee."
advice: Next time, bring the bike into the cafe. The barista will understand.

desc: "I will become a vigilante hero who hunts bike thieves in the night."
advice: Cancel the batarang order. You don't have the budget.

desc: "Remember that time we rode through the park and hit a pothole together? Good times."
advice: Write a poem about the pothole. It helps.

desc: "I guess this city really does require a sacrifice every few months."
advice: Pay your tribute and move on.

desc: "I don't need gears or brakes. I'm getting a unicycle."
advice: Also get knee pads. Lots of them.
